questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. It was inevitable that the rain would start just as we began our picnic, as the clouds had been gathering all morning.
questions.explanation: A) Inevitable(adjective) – Unavoidable, Certain, Inescapable, Unpreventable, Predetermined अपरिहार्य Antonym: Avoidable(adjective) – Able to be avoided or prevented. टालने योग्य Certain(adjective) – Known for sure; established beyond doubt. निश्चित Predetermined(adjective) – Established or decided in advance. पूर्व निर्धारित Inescapable(adjective) – Unable to be avoided or denied. अपरिहार्य

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. The science of teaching
questions.explanation: B)Pedagogy(noun) – The science of teaching शिक्षणशास्त्र

questions.question: In the following sentence the underlined part contains an error. Alternatives to the underlined part are given as options. Select the correct alternative. When they left for Mexico, their house was in good condition than it is now
questions.explanation: A)‘better' का use 'good' के Comparative Degree के रूप में किया जाएगा क्योंकि sentence में तुलना की जा रही है। 'Good' के Comparative Degree के लिए 'better' का use होता है। sentence का अर्थ है कि जब वे मैक्सिको गए, तो उनका घर अब की तुलना में बेहतर स्थिति में था। उदाहरण— The food is better today than it was yesterday. The word 'better' will be used as the comparative degree of 'good' because the sentence is making a comparison. For the comparative degree of 'good', the correct form is 'better'. The sentence means that when they left for Mexico, their house was in a better condition than it is now. Therefore, the correct answer is 'better'. For example— The food is better today than it was yesterday.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. The manager's decision to cut bonuses this year was met with displeasure from the employees, leading to widespread discontent.
questions.explanation: A) Displeasure(noun) – Dissatisfaction, Discontent, Annoyance, Irritation, Anger असंतोष Antonym: Contentment(noun) – A state of happiness and satisfaction, peace, pleasure. संतोषAnnoyance(noun) – The feeling or state of being annoyed, irritation, vexation. खिझIrritation(noun) – The state of feeling annoyed, impatient, or slightly angry, annoyance. चिढ़Anger(noun) – A strong feeling of annoyance, displeasure, or hostility, rage. क्रोध

questions.question: For the four-sentence (S1 to S4) paragraph below, sentences S1 and S4 are given. From the options P, Q, R and S select the appropriate sentences for S2 and S3, respectively. S1) The news about China’s economic slowdown has caused mixed reactions. P) China's sovereignty and maritime claims based on the nine-dotted line on the map are "invalid," said the statement, which cited Pham Thu Hang, the spokesperson for Vietnam's foreign affairs ministry Q) China released the map so publishers, companies and others have an official version to reference. R) China, for long, had been worried about fears of a slowdown and a middle-income trap. S) Now, there are fears of deflation which may bring bad news for China and the rest of the world. S4) Thus, understanding the causes and the magnitude of China’s present-day economic challenges is essential.
questions.explanation: C)R and S Based on the theme set by S1 and S4, sentences R and S (Option C) seem to be the most appropriate choices for S2 and S3 respectively. They provide context about China's longstanding concerns and the potential implications, fitting in with the theme of the economic slowdown. P: This sentence speaks about maritime claims, which does not fit the theme of economic slowdown. Q: This sentence also discusses a map release but does not directly relate to China's economic conditions. R: This sentence is about China's fears of an economic slowdown, which directly relates to the theme. S: This sentence discusses the potential economic consequences (deflation) for China and globally, which again fits the theme.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. I always prefer a small, cosy cottage ______ a high-rise apartment.
questions.explanation: C)To'का use होगा क्योंकि "prefer" के साथ जब दो चीजों की तुलना की जाती है, तो सही preposition 'to' होता है। Sentence में यह दर्शाया गया है कि "small, cosy cottage" को "high-rise apartment" पर प्राथमिकता दी जा रही है, इसलिए 'to' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'from' का अर्थ है किसी स्रोत या दूरी का उल्लेख करना, 'for' उद्देश्य के लिए use होता है, और 'than' का use comparative degree के लिए होता है, जो यहाँ सही नहीं है। 'To'will be used because with "prefer," when comparing two things, the correct preposition is 'to'. The sentence conveys that a "small, cosy cottage" is preferred over a "high-rise apartment," making 'to' appropriate here. Whereas 'from' refers to a source, 'for' is used for purpose, and 'than' is used with comparatives, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the word given in brackets to fill in the blank. Despite her exhaustion, Emily managed to maintain a(n) _____ (persistent) attitude throughout the day.
questions.explanation: C)tenacious(adjective) – persistent, determined, resolute, dogged, unyielding दृढ़

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the spelling error of the underlined word. Preserving our planet's biodiversity is essential for the health and susteinability of ecosystems and human life.
questions.explanation: B) The correct spelling of the underlined word "susteinability" is "sustainability," which means “the ability to be maintained at a certain rate or level, especially without depleting natural resources” (स्थिरता, टिकाऊपन).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Gandhi’s ways of life, if followed in their entirety, will aid us in the creation of a society of peace and _______.
questions.explanation: C) ‘Harmony’का use सही है क्योंकि इसका अर्थ होता है शांति, सौहार्द्र या सामंजस्य, जो गांधीजी के जीवन दर्शन के अनुसार एक शांतिपूर्ण और आदर्श समाज के निर्माण का संदर्भ देता है। यहाँ ‘peace’ (शांति) और ‘harmony’ एक दूसरे के पूरक हैं। Discord: इसका अर्थ होता है विवाद या असहमति, जो ‘peace’ के विपरीत है। इस संदर्भ में यह पूरी तरह से अनुचित है। Agitation: इसका अर्थ होता है व्याकुलता या अशांति, जो ‘peace’ के बिल्कुल विपरीत है। यह शब्द वाक्य की भावनात्मक और संदर्भगत संगति को बिगाड़ देता है।Cacophony: इसका अर्थ होता है कर्णकटु शोर या अव्यवस्थित ध्वनि, जो ‘peace’ के विपरीत अर्थ रखता है। ‘Harmony’is the correct choice because it means a state of peaceful coexistence and agreement, which aligns with the idea of a peaceful and ideal society as described in the sentence. The word “peace” and “harmony” complement each other, making this option contextually appropriate. Discord: It means disagreement or conflict, which is the opposite of “peace” and does not fit the positive tone of the sentence. Agitation: It refers to unrest or disturbance, which contradicts the concept of “peace” and is inappropriate here. Cacophony: It means a harsh, discordant noise, which again opposes the idea of a peaceful society.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined word in a sentence Her adroit management of the situation defused a potentially explosive argument
questions.explanation: B.Skillful

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: D) The correct spelling of "Calender" isCalendar, which refers to a chart or series of pages showing the days, weeks, and months of a particular year, or a schedule of events. In Hindi, it means पंचांग या तिथि तालिका.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A system of government by one person with absolute power
questions.explanation: D)Autocracy(noun) – A system of government by one person with absolute power एकतंत्र

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. The protagonist of the novel was known for hisperspicacity in solving complex problems
questions.explanation: C) Perspicacity(noun) – Intelligence, acuity, sharpness, कुशाग्र, बुद्धिसंपन्‍नता

questions.question: Select the most appropriate phrase that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The question paper was very easy to complete
questions.explanation: C)A piece of cake(Idiom)– Very easy to completeबहुतआसान A) The last straw – The final problem that makes a situation unbearableआखिरीऔरअसहनीयसमस्या B) Fair and square – In an honest and straightforward mannerईमानदारीऔरनिष्पक्षतासे D) A snowball effect – A situation that rapidly grows bigger or worseएकतेजीसेबढ़तीहुईस्थिति

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The train is running / late by six hours / so it / cannot make over the time.
questions.explanation: C) 'cannot make over the time' के बदले'cannot make up the time'का USE होगा क्योंकि 'make up time' का अर्थ होता है "खोए हुए समय की भरपाई करना", जबकि 'make over' का अर्थ होता है "किसी चीज़ का स्वामित्व बदलना या सुधार करना", जो यहाँ गलत USE है; जैसे— The train is running late by six hours, so it cannot make up the time. 'cannot make up the time' will be used instead of 'cannot make over the time' because 'make up time' means to compensate for lost time, whereas 'make over' means to transfer ownership or renovate something, which is incorrect in this context; Like— The train is running late by six hours, so it cannot make up the time

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The manager decided to disclose a secret and address the issue at the team meeting.
options: hit the nail on the head
questions.explanation: A)Spill the beans(idiom) – To reveal a secret or confidential information. राज़ खोलना या गुप्त जानकारी उजागर करना। Sweep it under the rug– Hide or ignore a problem समस्या को छिपाना या अनदेखा करना Bury the hatchet– End a conflict and make peace दुश्मनी खत्म कर सुलह करना Hit the nail on the head– Identify the exact problem समस्या की जड़ को पहचानना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank. We hear the judges from their ________ that only CNG buses should run in Delhi
questions.explanation: D) Ivory tower(noun) – A state of privileged seclusion or separation from the real world, often with a lack of practical concern. इसका मतलब है व्यावहारिक दुनिया से दूर, एकांत में रहना और वास्तविक समस्याओं से अनभिज्ञ रहना

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. He sings good / among all the / contestants in / the singing competition.
questions.explanation: D) ‘good' के बदले'well'का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँ 'sings' Verb को qualify कर रहा है और Verb को qualify करने के लिए Adverb का प्रयोग किया जाता है, जबकि 'good' एक Adjective है। Well का प्रयोग Adverb के रूप में होता है, जो यहाँ सही विकल्प है। जैसे— He plays well (वह अच्छा खेलता है)। 'well'will be used instead of 'good' because the word 'sings' is a Verb and to qualify a Verb, we use an Adverb. 'Good' is an Adjective, so it cannot modify the Verb 'sings'.For example: He plays well.

questions.question: Change the following sentence into positive degree. Ramesh is not stronger than Mahesh
options: Mahesh is as strong as Ramesh.
Ramesh is as strong than Mahesh.
questions.explanation: B) ‘Mahesh is as strong as Ramesh'का use होगा क्योंकि वाक्य को Positive Degree में बदलना है। Positive Degree के वाक्यों में 'as + Adjective + as' का useहोता है, जब दो व्यक्तियों या वस्तुओं की तुलना समानता में होती है। 'Mahesh is as strong as Ramesh'will be used because the sentence needs to be converted into Positive Degree. In Positive Degree sentences, the structure 'as + Adjective + as' is used to indicate equality between two people or objects.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P) Recently, there was a function to release a book written by an architect in Kerala. Q) Written in an easy style, the book was a pleasure to read. R) The book was a collection of memories of the author’s travels to exotic places and about his blissful moments watching nature. S) There was nothing about his personal background or achievements in life.
questions.explanation: B)PRSQ P: Sentence P begins with the word "Recently", indicating a starting point or introduction to an event. This suggests that P is the beginning of the paragraph. R: "The book" in R refers back to "a book" in P, making it clear that R should follow P. S: The pronoun "his" in S refers to "the author" in R, indicating that S should come after R to maintain the noun-pronoun relationship. Q: This is more of a judgment or a review about the book and makes sense to come after all the contents and what the book does or doesn't cover have been discussed. R and S pair is only in B option.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. “The Union has to activate itself,” Justice Hima Kohli, who is heading the Supreme Court Bench hearing the case against Patanjali Ayurved and its leaders Acharya Balkrishna and Baba Ramdev, observed on April 23 in the context of the government not having taken any action against the company for publishing advertisements _____1_____ untested, pseudoscientific cures for COVID-19, diabetes, and other conditions. The Bench also took cognisance of a report that the baby formula Nestlé sells in India contains more sugar than its corresponding product in Europe, and expanded the Patanjali Ayurved matter’s remit to include all fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) companies publishing misleading advertisements. India has been recording a surge in non-communicable diseases (NCDs) ____2____the easy availability of ultra-processed foods, together with _____3____lifestyles. Manufacturers have also been known to include some vitamins, say, in order to escape scrutiny, but their product is still ‘junk’. In the last month, the apex court has sought public ____4_____from Patanjali Ayurved et al. for advertising misleading claims even after the Court directed them to stop; the Bench ____5____ the defendants for publishing a diminutive advertisement. There is some uncertainty now over whether the Court will accept the latest apology, but herein lies the rub. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: D) 'Touting' का use होगा क्योंकि "touting" का अर्थ होता है किसी चीज़ को ज़ोर-शोर से प्रचारित करना। paragraph में mention है कि सरकार ने पतंजलि आयुर्वेद के खिलाफ कोई कदम नहीं उठाया है जबकि उसने COVID-19, मधुमेह और अन्य बीमारियों के लिए अनपरीक्षित, प्सेउडोसाइंटिफिक इलाजों का प्रचार किया है, इसलिए 'touting' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Disquieting' का अर्थ है चिंताजनक, 'Charting' का अर्थ है मार्गदर्शन करना, और 'Bloating' का अर्थ है फूलना या सूजना, जो इस context में सही नहीं हैं। 'Touting' will be used because it means to promote something vigorously. The paragraph describes how the government has not taken any action against Patanjali Ayurved despite their promotion of untested, pseudoscientific cures for COVID-19, diabetes, and other conditions, making 'touting' fitting here. Whereas, 'Disquieting' means causing anxiety or unease, 'Charting' means planning or mapping out, and 'Bloating' means swelling, none of which are suitable in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. “The Union has to activate itself,” Justice Hima Kohli, who is heading the Supreme Court Bench hearing the case against Patanjali Ayurved and its leaders Acharya Balkrishna and Baba Ramdev, observed on April 23 in the context of the government not having taken any action against the company for publishing advertisements _____1_____ untested, pseudoscientific cures for COVID-19, diabetes, and other conditions. The Bench also took cognisance of a report that the baby formula Nestlé sells in India contains more sugar than its corresponding product in Europe, and expanded the Patanjali Ayurved matter’s remit to include all fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) companies publishing misleading advertisements. India has been recording a surge in non-communicable diseases (NCDs) ____2____the easy availability of ultra-processed foods, together with _____3____lifestyles. Manufacturers have also been known to include some vitamins, say, in order to escape scrutiny, but their product is still ‘junk’. In the last month, the apex court has sought public ____4_____from Patanjali Ayurved et al. for advertising misleading claims even after the Court directed them to stop; the Bench ____5____ the defendants for publishing a diminutive advertisement. There is some uncertainty now over whether the Court will accept the latest apology, but herein lies the rub. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: A) 'thanks to' का use होगा क्योंकि "thanks to" का अर्थ होता है किसी कारण से या किसी चीज़ की वजह से। paragraph में यह mention है कि भारत में गैर-संचारी रोगों (NCDs) में वृद्धि दर्ज की जा रही है, जो अल्ट्रा-प्रोसेस्ड खाद्य पदार्थों की आसान उपलब्धता के कारण हो रही है, इसलिए 'thanks to' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'contrary to' का अर्थ है किसी बात के विपरीत, 'attention to' का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ पर ध्यान देना, और 'counter to' का अर्थ है किसी बात के खिलाफ, जो इस context में सही नहीं हैं। 'thanks to' will be used because it means because of something or due to something. The paragraph states that India has been recording a surge in non-communicable diseases (NCDs) due to the easy availability of ultra-processed foods, making 'thanks to' appropriate here. Whereas, 'contrary to' means opposite to, 'attention to' means focusing on something, and 'counter to' means against something, none of which fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. “The Union has to activate itself,” Justice Hima Kohli, who is heading the Supreme Court Bench hearing the case against Patanjali Ayurved and its leaders Acharya Balkrishna and Baba Ramdev, observed on April 23 in the context of the government not having taken any action against the company for publishing advertisements _____1_____ untested, pseudoscientific cures for COVID-19, diabetes, and other conditions. The Bench also took cognisance of a report that the baby formula Nestlé sells in India contains more sugar than its corresponding product in Europe, and expanded the Patanjali Ayurved matter’s remit to include all fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) companies publishing misleading advertisements. India has been recording a surge in non-communicable diseases (NCDs) ____2____the easy availability of ultra-processed foods, together with _____3____lifestyles. Manufacturers have also been known to include some vitamins, say, in order to escape scrutiny, but their product is still ‘junk’. In the last month, the apex court has sought public ____4_____from Patanjali Ayurved et al. for advertising misleading claims even after the Court directed them to stop; the Bench ____5____ the defendants for publishing a diminutive advertisement. There is some uncertainty now over whether the Court will accept the latest apology, but herein lies the rub. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: C) 'Sedentary'का use होगा क्योंकि "sedentary" का अर्थ होता है बहुत कम शारीरिक गतिविधि वाली जीवनशैली। paragraph में यह mention है कि भारत में गैर-संचारी रोगों (NCDs) की वृद्धि अल्ट्रा-प्रोसेस्ड खाद्य पदार्थों की आसान उपलब्धता के साथ-साथ कम गतिविधि वाली जीवनशैली के कारण हो रही है, इसलिए 'sedentary' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Uncomplimentary' का अर्थ है अप्रशंसनीय, 'Elementary' का अर्थ है मूलभूत या सरल, और 'Primary' का अर्थ है प्राथमिक, जो इस context में सही नहीं हैं। 'Sedentary' will be used because it refers to a lifestyle characterized by a lot of sitting or very little physical activity. The paragraph mentions the surge in non-communicable diseases (NCDs) in India due to the easy availability of ultra-processed foods, coupled with sedentary lifestyles, making 'sedentary' the right choice. Whereas, 'Uncomplimentary' means not flattering, 'Elementary' means basic or simple, and 'Primary' means first in order, none of which are suitable in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. “The Union has to activate itself,” Justice Hima Kohli, who is heading the Supreme Court Bench hearing the case against Patanjali Ayurved and its leaders Acharya Balkrishna and Baba Ramdev, observed on April 23 in the context of the government not having taken any action against the company for publishing advertisements _____1_____ untested, pseudoscientific cures for COVID-19, diabetes, and other conditions. The Bench also took cognisance of a report that the baby formula Nestlé sells in India contains more sugar than its corresponding product in Europe, and expanded the Patanjali Ayurved matter’s remit to include all fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) companies publishing misleading advertisements. India has been recording a surge in non-communicable diseases (NCDs) ____2____the easy availability of ultra-processed foods, together with _____3____lifestyles. Manufacturers have also been known to include some vitamins, say, in order to escape scrutiny, but their product is still ‘junk’. In the last month, the apex court has sought public ____4_____from Patanjali Ayurved et al. for advertising misleading claims even after the Court directed them to stop; the Bench ____5____ the defendants for publishing a diminutive advertisement. There is some uncertainty now over whether the Court will accept the latest apology, but herein lies the rub. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4
questions.explanation: B) 'Apologies'का use होगा क्योंकि "apologies" का अर्थ होता है माफ़ी या क्षमायाचना। paragraph में mention है कि सुप्रीम कोर्ट ने पतंजलि आयुर्वेद से पब्लिक अपोलॉजी माँगी है क्योंकि उन्होंने कोर्ट के आदेश के बाद भी भ्रामक दावों का प्रचार किया था, इसलिए 'apologies' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Series' का अर्थ है श्रृंखला, 'Responsibilities' का अर्थ है जिम्मेदारियाँ, और 'Approaches' का अर्थ है दृष्टिकोण या पद्धति, जो इस context में सही नहीं हैं। 'Apologies' will be used because it refers to expressions of regret or asking for forgiveness. The paragraph mentions that the apex court has sought public apologies from Patanjali Ayurved for advertising misleading claims even after the court directed them to stop, making 'apologies' fitting here. Whereas, 'Series' means a number of similar or related events or things, one following another, 'Responsibilities' means duties or obligations, and 'Approaches' means methods or ways, none of which fit in this context

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. “The Union has to activate itself,” Justice Hima Kohli, who is heading the Supreme Court Bench hearing the case against Patanjali Ayurved and its leaders Acharya Balkrishna and Baba Ramdev, observed on April 23 in the context of the government not having taken any action against the company for publishing advertisements _____1_____ untested, pseudoscientific cures for COVID-19, diabetes, and other conditions. The Bench also took cognisance of a report that the baby formula Nestlé sells in India contains more sugar than its corresponding product in Europe, and expanded the Patanjali Ayurved matter’s remit to include all fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) companies publishing misleading advertisements. India has been recording a surge in non-communicable diseases (NCDs) ____2____the easy availability of ultra-processed foods, together with _____3____lifestyles. Manufacturers have also been known to include some vitamins, say, in order to escape scrutiny, but their product is still ‘junk’. In the last month, the apex court has sought public ____4_____from Patanjali Ayurved et al. for advertising misleading claims even after the Court directed them to stop; the Bench ____5____ the defendants for publishing a diminutive advertisement. There is some uncertainty now over whether the Court will accept the latest apology, but herein lies the rub. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5
questions.explanation: A) 'Chided'का use होगा क्योंकि "chided" का अर्थ होता है डांटना या निंदा करना। paragraph में बताया गया है कि बेंच ने प्रतिवादियों की निंदा की क्योंकि उन्होंने एक छोटे विज्ञापन का प्रकाशन किया था। इसलिए 'chided' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Sealed' का अर्थ है सील करना, 'Immobilized' का अर्थ है अस्थिर करना या रोकना, और 'Monitored' का अर्थ है निगरानी करना, जो इस context में सही नहीं हैं। 'Chided' will be used because it means to scold or rebuke. The paragraph describes how the Bench rebuked the defendants for publishing a diminutive advertisement, making 'chided' the appropriate choice. Whereas, 'Sealed' means to close securely, 'Immobilized' means to prevent from moving, and 'Monitored' means to observe or supervise, none of which are suitable in this context
